0: manipulate ex-vivo tissue to achieve a desired exposure (usually uncover an interface or object), using bimanual grasping, lifting/pulling, and regrasping as needed
1: perform a simple interrupted stitch on ex-vivo tissue (occasionally on silicon suturing training phantom) ; without cutting the suture thread tail at the end
2: perform cold-cut dissection on ex-vivo tissue by cutting along pre-defined traces using non-energy surigal instruments
3: perform peg transfer on a table-top phantom by moving triangular-prism rubber objects between pegs, using bimanual grasping and handoffs as needed
4: grasp and move ex-vivo tissues toward a specified configuration (usually move to a certain area), using lift-pull-release/regrasp actions while avoiding excessive stretching or tearing
5: perform cold-cut dissection by cutting through pork intestines using non-energy surgical instruments; no pre-defined cutting path
6: perform cold-cut dissection to partially peel skin from the underlying fat layer using non-energy instruments
